algorithms.title: JumpSTART Pediatric MCI Triage
algorithms.description: Sequence for triaging a pediatric patient at a mass casualty incident using the JumpSTART algorithm.
steps: Assess ability to walk. If the child can walk, assign Minor (green) — but note that very young children may not yet walk independently at baseline, so use judgment for infants/toddlers.
If not walking, assess breathing. If not breathing, reposition the airway and reassess.
If breathing resumes after repositioning, assign Immediate (red).
If still not breathing and no pulse is present, assign Expectant (black/gray).
If still not breathing but a pulse is present, give five rescue breaths, then reassess.
If breathing resumes after rescue breaths, assign Immediate (red). If still apneic, assign Expectant (black/gray).
If breathing spontaneously, assess respiratory rate using pediatric-specific thresholds for age. If abnormal, assign Immediate (red).
If respiratory rate is normal for age, assess perfusion (peripheral pulse presence, capillary refill) and mental status using AVPU.
If perfusion or AVPU is abnormal, assign Immediate (red). If both are normal, assign Delayed (yellow).
Continue reassessment as resources and scene conditions allow.

algorithms.title: Pediatric Transport Destination Decision
algorithms.description: Sequence for determining appropriate transport destination for a pediatric patient.
steps: Assess the severity and type of the child's condition.
Identify nearby facilities and their pediatric-specific capabilities (pediatric trauma center, PICU, pediatric surgical subspecialists).
Apply local protocol criteria for pediatric-capable/pediatric trauma center bypass.
Consult medical direction if the decision is unclear or the situation falls outside routine protocol.
Transport to the destination indicated by protocol, even if a closer general facility is available, when bypass criteria are met.
